title: Technology Basics
description: Question batch for the Computer Fundamentals domain.

questions.question: Which statement best describes CPU?
answers: The main processor that executes instructions.
Fast storage that uses flash memory and has no moving parts.
Storage that saves data on spinning magnetic disks.
Core software that manages hardware and applications.
questions.explanation: CPU is the main processor that executes instructions.

questions.question: What does RAM refer to?
answers: Short-term memory used by active programs.
Storage that saves data on spinning magnetic disks.
Core software that manages hardware and applications.
The main circuit board that connects computer components.
questions.explanation: RAM is short-term memory used by active programs.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is SSD?
answers: Fast storage that uses flash memory and has no moving parts.
Core software that manages hardware and applications.
The main circuit board that connects computer components.
A processor specialized for graphics and parallel visual tasks.
questions.explanation: SSD is fast storage that uses flash memory and has no moving parts.

questions.question: Which statement best describes HDD?
answers: Storage that saves data on spinning magnetic disks.
The main circuit board that connects computer components.
A processor specialized for graphics and parallel visual tasks.
The component that converts electricity for internal hardware.
questions.explanation: HDD is storage that saves data on spinning magnetic disks.

questions.question: What does Operating system refer to?
answers: Core software that manages hardware and applications.
A processor specialized for graphics and parallel visual tasks.
The component that converts electricity for internal hardware.
The screen that displays visual output from a computer.
questions.explanation: Operating system is core software that manages hardware and applications.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Motherboard?
answers: The main circuit board that connects computer components.
The component that converts electricity for internal hardware.
The screen that displays visual output from a computer.
An input device used to type text and commands.
questions.explanation: Motherboard is the main circuit board that connects computer components.

questions.question: Which statement best describes GPU?
answers: A processor specialized for graphics and parallel visual tasks.
The screen that displays visual output from a computer.
An input device used to type text and commands.
A pointing device used to move the cursor and click items.
questions.explanation: GPU is a processor specialized for graphics and parallel visual tasks.

questions.question: What does Power supply refer to?
answers: The component that converts electricity for internal hardware.
An input device used to type text and commands.
A pointing device used to move the cursor and click items.
A common connector used for peripherals, charging, and data transfer.
questions.explanation: Power supply is the component that converts electricity for internal hardware.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Monitor?
answers: The screen that displays visual output from a computer.
A pointing device used to move the cursor and click items.
A common connector used for peripherals, charging, and data transfer.
A named collection of stored data on a device.
questions.explanation: Monitor is the screen that displays visual output from a computer.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Keyboard?
answers: An input device used to type text and commands.
A common connector used for peripherals, charging, and data transfer.
A named collection of stored data on a device.
A container used to organize files and other folders.
questions.explanation: Keyboard is an input device used to type text and commands.

questions.question: What does Mouse refer to?
answers: A pointing device used to move the cursor and click items.
A named collection of stored data on a device.
A container used to organize files and other folders.
Software designed to help a user perform a specific task.
questions.explanation: Mouse is a pointing device used to move the cursor and click items.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is USB port?
answers: A common connector used for peripherals, charging, and data transfer.
A container used to organize files and other folders.
Software designed to help a user perform a specific task.
Software used to open and navigate websites.
questions.explanation: USB port is a common connector used for peripherals, charging, and data transfer.

questions.question: Which statement best describes File?
answers: A named collection of stored data on a device.
Software designed to help a user perform a specific task.
Software used to open and navigate websites.
The act of restarting a device or operating system.
questions.explanation: File is a named collection of stored data on a device.

questions.question: What does Folder refer to?
answers: A container used to organize files and other folders.
Software used to open and navigate websites.
The act of restarting a device or operating system.
A software release that improves features, fixes bugs, or patches issues.
questions.explanation: Folder is a container used to organize files and other folders.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Application?
answers: Software designed to help a user perform a specific task.
The act of restarting a device or operating system.
A software release that improves features, fixes bugs, or patches issues.
A copy of data kept so it can be restored later.
questions.explanation: Application is software designed to help a user perform a specific task.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Browser?
answers: Software used to open and navigate websites.
A software release that improves features, fixes bugs, or patches issues.
A copy of data kept so it can be restored later.
Online storage accessed through the internet instead of only one local device.
questions.explanation: Browser is software used to open and navigate websites.

questions.question: What does Reboot refer to?
answers: The act of restarting a device or operating system.
A copy of data kept so it can be restored later.
Online storage accessed through the internet instead of only one local device.
A basic data unit made of eight bits.
questions.explanation: Reboot is the act of restarting a device or operating system.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Update?
answers: A software release that improves features, fixes bugs, or patches issues.
Online storage accessed through the internet instead of only one local device.
A basic data unit made of eight bits.
The smallest unit of digital information, represented as 0 or 1.
questions.explanation: Update is a software release that improves features, fixes bugs, or patches issues.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Backup?
answers: A copy of data kept so it can be restored later.
A basic data unit made of eight bits.
The smallest unit of digital information, represented as 0 or 1.
The number of pixels shown on a display image.
questions.explanation: Backup is a copy of data kept so it can be restored later.

questions.question: What does Cloud storage refer to?
answers: Online storage accessed through the internet instead of only one local device.
The smallest unit of digital information, represented as 0 or 1.
The number of pixels shown on a display image.
A display that also accepts finger or stylus input.
questions.explanation: Cloud storage is online storage accessed through the internet instead of only one local device.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Byte?
answers: A basic data unit made of eight bits.
The number of pixels shown on a display image.
A display that also accepts finger or stylus input.
An input device that captures sound.
questions.explanation: Byte is a basic data unit made of eight bits.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Bit?
answers: The smallest unit of digital information, represented as 0 or 1.
A display that also accepts finger or stylus input.
An input device that captures sound.
An output device that plays sound.
questions.explanation: Bit is the smallest unit of digital information, represented as 0 or 1.

questions.question: What does Resolution refer to?
answers: The number of pixels shown on a display image.
An input device that captures sound.
An output device that plays sound.
A device that produces physical copies of digital content.
questions.explanation: Resolution is the number of pixels shown on a display image.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Touchscreen?
answers: A display that also accepts finger or stylus input.
An output device that plays sound.
A device that produces physical copies of digital content.
A camera used to capture video through a computer or mobile device.
questions.explanation: Touchscreen is a display that also accepts finger or stylus input.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Microphone?
answers: An input device that captures sound.
A device that produces physical copies of digital content.
A camera used to capture video through a computer or mobile device.
A component that stores power for portable electronic devices.
questions.explanation: Microphone is an input device that captures sound.

questions.question: What does Speaker refer to?
answers: An output device that plays sound.
A camera used to capture video through a computer or mobile device.
A component that stores power for portable electronic devices.
The total amount of data a device can hold.
questions.explanation: Speaker is an output device that plays sound.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Printer?
answers: A device that produces physical copies of digital content.
A component that stores power for portable electronic devices.
The total amount of data a device can hold.
The main processor that executes instructions.
questions.explanation: Printer is a device that produces physical copies of digital content.

questions.question: Which statement best describes Webcam?
answers: A camera used to capture video through a computer or mobile device.
The total amount of data a device can hold.
The main processor that executes instructions.
Short-term memory used by active programs.
questions.explanation: Webcam is a camera used to capture video through a computer or mobile device.

questions.question: What does Battery refer to?
answers: A component that stores power for portable electronic devices.
The main processor that executes instructions.
Short-term memory used by active programs.
Fast storage that uses flash memory and has no moving parts.
questions.explanation: Battery is a component that stores power for portable electronic devices.

questions.question: In basic technology, what is Storage capacity?
answers: The total amount of data a device can hold.
Short-term memory used by active programs.
Fast storage that uses flash memory and has no moving parts.
Storage that saves data on spinning magnetic disks.
questions.explanation: Storage capacity is the total amount of data a device can hold.
